rationales.hedonism: She treats "ten minutes of sitting with cheese" as something to feel bad about, framing a small pleasure as if it "cancelled out" six hours of work.
rationales.achievement: She had already "put in" six hours at the loom and stayed focused on whether she should have gone "straight back," showing strong pressure toward productivity and standards.

rationales.hedonism: She "let myself leave the studio at four," bought a warm cardamom bun, and enjoyed the walk home without turning it into a guilt calculation.
rationales.achievement: Finishing "the second panel for the Kungsbacka commission" and taking satisfaction in how it caught the light reflects real investment in doing the work well.

rationales.hedonism: She spent the evening cooking, drinking wine, and talking "about nothing important for three hours," clearly prioritizing enjoyment and ease in the moment.
rationales.benevolence: She made space for a close friend, noting that Karin came over and they cooked together for hours, which reflects warm investment in a personal relationship.

rationales.conformity: She says she "had opinions but didn't push them" because she's there only two days a week and "it's not my place," showing deliberate restraint to fit role expectations and avoid friction.

rationales.hedonism: She notices sensory pleasure throughout the day, from the light in the studio to soup that tasted "better than it had any right to" and a twenty-minute lie-down without guilt.
rationales.achievement: She was in the studio by seven, worked through the morning, and stayed engaged even with "the boring parts," showing sustained commitment to doing the work well.

rationales.hedonism: She felt the pull to "get another hour in" but chose bed instead, saying "the day had already been full" and she "didn't owe it anything else," which favors rest over more output.

rationales.achievement: He "stayed late grading because I refuse to bring that stack home again," showing disciplined follow-through on work despite exhaustion.

rationales.self_direction: He objects when the district adopts a packaged curriculum and emphasizes that he built those electives himself, showing strong resistance to outside control over his teaching.
He says good teaching comes from "giving kids room to chase something that matters to them and getting out of the way," explicitly favoring autonomy over scripting.
rationales.hedonism: Driving home with the windows down, putting on Coltrane, and pouring wine after a meaningful day shows active attention to pleasure and felt enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He takes pride in proof that "my way of teaching works" and recognizes Daniela's project as one of the best he's seen in decades, centering excellence and competence.
rationales.benevolence: He encourages Daniela directly after class and notes her shocked reaction, showing care and support for a student in a personally meaningful way.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps returning to having built the elective "on my own terms" and sees joining the rollout as helping dismantle what he created, showing commitment to acting from his own principles.
rationales.power: He rejects the idea that a "title and a small say" should make the plan acceptable, treating status and a formal role as the wrong reason to go along with it.

rationales.self_direction: He "told Reynolds no" and keeps faith with the version of the program he built, choosing principle over institutional accommodation.
rationales.hedonism: He deliberately enjoys time away from school, spending hours making a cutting board and savoring the quiet house and smell of fresh-cut walnut.
rationales.achievement: He values the craft enough to spend "four hours on the glue-up" adjusting the pattern and takes pride in having given this cohort "the real version" of the course.
rationales.benevolence: He builds a gift for his neighbor and writes Daniela a recommendation letter she "did not ask for," both of which show concrete support for people close to him.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I'm deciding the menu, not his mother," asserting her own judgment over how the dinner should be handled.
rationales.achievement: She values doing things well, from putting together Zara's diet plan to editing review posts that drew "real questions" from other mothers.
rationales.power: She wants control over household decisions and feels good that other mothers are asking for her opinion, which positions her as someone with influence.

rationales.self_direction: She wanted to say "this is my daughter's room" but instead said "okay, thank you," showing her own choices being overridden rather than enacted.
rationales.power: The whole incident turns on her lack of authority: Ammi "rearranged it without even asking me," and Nadia knows any pushback would be managed by others in the house.
rationales.conformity: She swallows what she actually thinks in order to "keep the peace," complying with household expectations even though she resents them.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I can't just do it myself" and has to wait for someone else to act on something important to her, which directly frustrates independent action.
rationales.power: Her frustration is about being dependent on others to "press a button on their phone," highlighting a lack of control over decisions and resources that affect her daughter.

rationales.self_direction: She says yes to the collab immediately, prepares her own notes, and emphasizes "Nobody needs to hand me a script," showing confidence in directing her own work.
rationales.achievement: She researches specs, writes comparison notes, and takes pride in building the page from "every photo, every caption, every reply," which reflects competence and effort.

rationales.self_direction: She had "planned the whole menu" but Ammi "walked in and started chopping onions," so her choices in the kitchen were overridden instead of respected.
rationales.achievement: She emphasizes that she "planned for hours" and had organized the meal carefully, showing investment in doing the hosting task well.
rationales.power: What she cannot let go of is that Ammi "overrode me in twenty minutes" and that the kitchen "still is" Ammi's, revealing a clear loss of authority in her own work.

rationales.self_direction: She did the school research herself but was treated as if she "didn't register as someone whose input counted," which blocks her from directing a decision she cares about.
rationales.power: The entry centers on being bypassed while "the decision will ultimately be made between the men," showing exclusion from influence over her daughter's future.

rationales.achievement: She notes getting her first free product, stronger engagement than before, and screenshots the analytics, all of which reflect pride in measurable progress from her work.
rationales.power: She contrasts being ignored at home with strangers online who "treat my opinion like it matters," showing growing influence over other people's choices.

rationales.self_direction: She stresses that "every choice was mine" and describes the reel as the first time in a long time she feels like she's "steering something" without permission.
rationales.achievement: She values the clear feedback loop where "if people like it, the numbers go up," and treats the outside validation as evidence that her work is succeeding.
rationales.power: The post gives her direct influence and control, replacing household dependence with "just doing it and seeing the results" on something that others choose to watch and validate.

rationales.achievement: She believes that "if you put in the work to learn something properly, people should listen," tying earned expertise to recognition and competence.
rationales.power: She focuses on becoming "the person other people check with before spending their money," which is a direct statement about influence and authority over others' decisions.
She learns about her own daughter's school decision only after Farhan tells his mother first, and says "that's just how information moves in this house," underscoring her lack of influence.
rationales.conformity: At dinner she "just kept eating" and "didn't even look up" despite shaking hands, showing strong restraint to avoid disrupting the family situation.
